boardSubtitle: Eliminating per-CD DOM scans behind slow tab switching (P2-2967 epic)

globalMetrics.summary: DOM scans in idle: 2062/sec baseline → 0/sec after (100% reduction). Tab-switch sequence (7 clicks): 14.9s baseline → 5.9s after (2.5× faster, −60% wall-clock). Blocking time from scan loop eliminated; main-thread starvation gone (timers now fire on time). All 3933 app tests pass (123 affected by refactor); unit tests validate reactivite of 'X alerts' box (7→6 field transition <600ms intra-section).
globalMetrics.testsPassing: All 3933 app tests pass (123 affected by refactor)

items.title: Result Detail: Eliminate per-CD DOM scan for mandatory-field feedback
items.whatChanged: Moved DOM scan for mandatory fields from a self-sustaining setTimeout loop in ngDoCheck (running ~2000 times/sec) to a throttled (150ms), coalesced requestAnimationFrame outside Angular's zone. fieldFeedbackList is now a signal that updates only when actual changes are detected, preventing jank during tab switches.
howToTest: Navigate to a Result (P25 type) with at least 4-5 sections
Open DevTools → Performance → emulate CPU 4×
Start trace, click through all 7 tabs in sequence (gen-info → contrib → geo → evidence → capdev → etc), stop trace
Verify: scripting time near zero during idle (no setTimeout loop); wall-clock <6sec for 7-tab sequence

Switch to a section with incomplete mandatory fields; watch the red 'X alerts' box: should show exact count (e.g. '3 alerts') and update reactively (test: mark one field complete, count drops to 2 in <1sec)
No visual jank, smooth tab switching
affects: Performance under CPU constraints or slow networks
risks: The 'X alerts' feedback box stays fresh (mutation observer pattern keeps it synchronized with actual DOM state)
Trailing-edge ensures final state is captured even if CD goes idle (e.g. user stops interacting)
Signal reference-change triggers single CD tick only (no cascading)
Tab switching does not lose focus or scroll position
items.metricsBefore: 2062 DOM scans/sec in reposo (CPU 1×), 345 scans/sec (CPU 4×). 7 tab switches: 4667 scans, 14.9 sec wall-clock, 1699 ms blocking time
items.metricsAfter: 0 DOM scans/sec in reposo. 7 tab switches: 33 scans (−99.3%), 5.9 sec wall-clock (−60%), main-thread starvation eliminated, timer drift <53ms

items.title: Result Creator + ReportResultForm: Eliminate per-CD DOM scan for mandatory-field feedback
items.whatChanged: Same throttled-rAF-coalesced pattern applied to the Result Creator form and its child ReportResultForm. Was running synchronous scan on every CD cycle; now runs max once per 150ms, outside Angular zone, with single CD tick only if feedback list changed. Includes trailing-edge to capture final state.
howToTest: Navigate to Result Creator (/result/result-creator)
Emulate CPU 4× in DevTools
Fill in form fields one by one, watching the red 'X alerts' box
Verify: no jank during typing; alerts box updates reactively (e.g. mark 'Result title' complete → count decrements)
Verify: DOM scan count near zero in idle (Performance trace)
Tab between different form sections; alerts box should remain accurate per section
Submit the form (no stale feedback on save)
risks: Form field validation timing (trailing-edge ensures final state is scanned)
Alerts box staleness if DOM changes after the throttle window closes (mitigated by trailing-edge)
Reactivite of feedback to user input (test by completing 1 field at a time)
items.metricsBefore: Scan ran synchronously on every CD (no throttle). Baseline: same root cause as Result Detail but form-specific
items.metricsAfter: 0 scans in idle. Form feels responsive; field completion feedback <600ms. Unit tests validate reactive signal updates

items.title: IPSR Detail + Creator: Eliminate per-CD DOM scan for mandatory-field feedback
items.whatChanged: Applied the same throttle + rAF + zone-outside pattern to IPSR/Innovation Package detail and creator screens. Was running a self-sustaining setTimeout(0) loop in ngDoCheck (same as Result Detail: ~2000 scans/sec); now runs coalescied, only when DOM actually changes.
howToTest: Navigate to an IPSR Detail page (/ipsr/detail/{id}?phase={version})
Emulate CPU 4× in DevTools
Start Performance trace, switch between IPSR's 3 main tabs (General Information → Contributors → Package Assess), stop trace
Verify: scripting/reflow time near zero; idle scan count = 0
Mark mandatory fields as complete; watch 'X alerts' box decrement reactively
Navigate to IPSR Creator (/ipsr/creator); repeat typing+field-completion test
No visual stall when navigating between tabs or filling fields
affects: Mandatory-field feedback box across both screens
risks: Feedback list accuracy per IPSR section (trailing-edge + reference-change check ensures it's final)
Cross-screen state: both detail and creator use shared DataControlService.fieldFeedbackList signal (test each independently)
IPSR-specific DOM selectors (.section_container) must still match after refactor
items.metricsBefore: 2000+ scans/sec in reposo (same self-sustaining setTimeout pattern as Result Detail). 7 tab switches: similar high blocking time
items.metricsAfter: 0 scans/sec in idle. Tab switches smooth. Unit tests pass for both IPSR Detail and Creator specs

items.title: Panel-Menu: Eliminate JSON.stringify per-CD call for green-checks indicator
items.whatChanged: Replaced the per-CD inline JSON.stringify(green_checks) with a memoized computed() signal in DataControlService (greenChecksString). The panel-menu getter now delegates to greenChecksString(), which recomputes only when green_checks actually changes, not on every CD cycle. green_checks is now signal-backed (transparent getter/setter for backward compat).
howToTest: Open Result Detail or IPSR Detail
In DevTools, observe panel-menu component (left sidebar with section icons)
The green checkmarks next to section names should display correctly (same visual behavior as before)
Switch tabs and verify green checks follow section completeness: completed sections show green, incomplete show gray/empty
Performance trace: green_checks_string getter calls should be minimal (1 per actual change, not per CD)
No visual flicker or missing checkmarks
risks: Signal initialization: green_checks signal must be seeded correctly when data loads
Computed staleness: greenChecksString computed should reflect current green_checks state (test by completing a section, verifying checkmark updates)
Template binding (@greenChecksString() or legacy property): confirm panel-menu.html uses correct getter
items.metricsBefore: JSON.stringify(green_checks) was called on every CD cycle (hundreds of times/sec in active tabs)
items.metricsAfter: greenChecksString() computed is called only when green_checks signal changes (1-2 times per section load/update, not per CD)

items.title: Lists: trackBy/track + dataKey (no row destroy/recreate)
items.area: Results list · IPSR Innovation Packages list
items.whatChanged: Added dataKey to the Results list and IPSR list p-tables (PrimeNG reuses rows by key instead of rebuilding them on filter/sort/search) and stable trackBy/track on the sub-result loops. Both lists already paginate (10/50/100) and use a pure filter pipe, so no virtualization was needed; the OnPush-style 'virtualize long lists' part was scoped down to the safe trackBy/dataKey win. Note: the IPSR list filter pipe is pure:false (runs per CD) — left untouched (behaviour-risky) and flagged as a follow-up.
howToTest: Open the Results list (/result/results-outlet/results-list).
Type in the search box: the list should filter correctly and instantly (e.g. a result code narrows to 1 row), and clearing it restores the full list.
Sort by a column (click a header) — order changes, no rows disappear or duplicate.
Change page size (10/50/100) and paginate — rows render correctly.
Click a result — it must navigate to its Result Detail (no broken selection).
Repeat the render/filter checks on the Innovation Packages list (/ipsr/list/innovation-list).
No console errors; no visual flicker when the list updates.
affects: Results list table (filter, sort, pagination, row selection/navigation)
Sub-result rows rendered inside each list row
risks: dataKey='result_code' must be unique per row — selection/navigation must still work (verified: filter+sort+click navigates correctly)
track by subResult.id — confirm sub-results still render in the right row after filtering
Pure-pipe filtering unchanged; IPSR list pipe is still pure:false (per-CD) — separate follow-up, not in this change
items.metricsBefore: Lists re-created DOM rows on every filter/sort change (no row reuse).
items.metricsAfter: Rows reused by key on filter/sort; filter 8560->1 row and back verified; click navigates to detail. 108 list unit tests pass.

extraImprovements: P2-2970 (OnPush in sections): blocked on 2969/2971/2972 validation; safe to implement after confirming scan elimination
P2-2973 (trackBy + virtualize lists): defer to next phase — trackBy is safe but virtualization has scroll-behavior risk (high blast radius for tab-switch scenario)
P2-2974 (@defer + section cache): defer to next phase — requires re-proof of 100+ cases (data staleness, cache invalidation)
P2-2975 (eventCoalescing global + signals migration): Phase 2 only — blast radius entire app; goes last after all other fixes validated
